paper.title_en: Vortex-Number-Controlled Josephson Diode Polarity in Corbino Junctions
paper.abstract_en: We demonstrate that the polarity of the Josephson diode effect in Corbino Josephson junctions can be deterministically controlled by the Josephson-vortex number, which arises as a generic consequence of structured spatial inhomogeneity. By solving the continuum Andreev spectral problem, we identify a mechanism in which the vortex number selectively filters specific spatial Fourier harmonics of the local inhomogeneity. This harmonic selection reshapes the amplitudes and relative phases of higher-order Josephson current harmonics, ultimately reversing the critical-current asymmetry. Numerical simulations of both an effective one-dimensional edge model and full two-dimensional lattice models confirm the robustness of this mechanism. Crucially, our results show that a vortex-parity-dependent reversal of diode polarity is not an exclusive signature of Majorana physics, but can emerge generically from geometric and structural inhomogeneities in Corbino junctions.
